text:
You are working on beads issue str-jxs0: "Extract Z3 constraint solving into Z3Solver strategy"

## Issue Details
Extract Z3 constraint solving from orchestrator.rs into a Z3Solver strategy struct implementing the InputStrategy trait. This is a reactive strategy that produces inputs by negating path constraints. feedback() records symbolic constraints from new paths. next() yields from internal queue of solved inputs. Infinite (produces work while unsolved constraints exist), but may have empty queue between feedback cycles. Wraps existing logic from orchestrator.rs.

Depends on str-5jx8 (InputStrategy trait - done).
Blocked by this: str-djqh (adaptive MetaStrategy).
Parent epic: str-7stb (Input strategy decomposition).

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="str-jxs0"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main.

## Instructions
1. Follow CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions
2. Read the existing InputStrategy trait definition (from str-5jx8) to understand the interface
3. Read orchestrator.rs to find the Z3 constraint solving logic to extract
4. Implement Z3Solver strategy with next()/feedback() interface
5. Add unit tests and proptest coverage for core invariants
6. Run quality gates: `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` in shatter-core
7. Run `/pre-completion` before reporting done — include its output in your completion message
8. Push your branch: `git push -u origin HEAD`

When done, mark task #1 as completed via TaskUpdate and send a message to the team lead.

Extract Z3 constraint solving from orchestrator.rs into a Z3Solver strategy struct implementing InputStrategy trait. Wraps existing logic from orchestrator.rs lines 395-424. feedback() records symbolic constraints from new paths. next() yields from internal queue of solved inputs. Depends on str-5jx8 (done). Run /pre-completion before reporting done.","assignedBy":"str-jxs0","timestamp":"2026-03-07T23:30:28.564Z"}

{"type":"permission_response","request_id":"perm-1772926858070-rbqirmo","subtype":"success","response":{"updated_input":{"command":"git add shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs shatter-core/src/strategy.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: extract Z3 constraint solving into Z3SolverStrategy (str-jxs0)

Add Z3SolverStrategy implementing InputStrategy trait. Reactive strategy
that negates path constraints via Z3 and queues solved inputs.

- feedback() extracts symbolic constraints, solves each via Z3, overlays
  solutions onto base inputs, queues in pending
- next() drains pending queue
- Infinite strategy (estimated_size returns None)
- Make extract_sym_constraints, concrete_to_json, overlay_solved_values
  pub(crate) in orchestrator.rs for reuse

Includes unit tests (7) and proptest properties (2: crash resistance,
length preservation).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"","description":"Commit changes"},"permission_updates":[]}}

{"type":"permission_response","request_id":"perm-1772926865179-khxrpwt","subtype":"success","response":{"updated_input":{"command":"git push -u origin HEAD","description":"Push branch to origin"},"permission_updates":[]}}
